blocks.content: The Pride of 1 o'clock frowned deeply.

blocks.speaker: Pride of 1 o'clock
blocks.content: Ugh!
Before the shockwave could tear Havitz apart, it was restored to its previous state by her intervention, reversing the choice made in the past.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: This is dangerous.
Because something created from nothing has no cause, the time to judge and respond was fleeting.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: If only the Sloth of 4 o'clock were here...
But what could be done?
A comrade had been shoved to their death by their own companions.
Hwoo-o-o-o
As Wizard succeeded in her preemptive strike, the shimmering mirage of the Void God rose again from her body.
The branches twisted into even more grotesque forms, and the long tongue writhed like an eel.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Now! Attack…!
As Siok shouted, the form of the Void God once again dissolved into the air.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Negative Perspective. Converging World.
Wizard's hand signs grew more intricate, and secondary and tertiary shockwaves erupted around Siok.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Guhhhh!
The Pride of 1 o'clock endured using a hidden code, but the strikes grew faster and faster.

blocks.speaker: Prejudice of 5 o'clock
blocks.content: Attack now! We can't hold on any longer!
The Prejudice of 5 o'clock yelled.

blocks.speaker: Prejudice of 5 o'clock
blocks.content: You're nothing but an insect! A worthless insect who can do nothing!
They thought it was over.
Even if she denied it, the Stubbornness of 3 o'clock would counter her claims.
The Revenge of 10 o'clock cast curses, and the Delusion of 9 o'clock dragged Wizard into an illusion.
And Wizard's response to all this?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Void God.
She merely reintegrated the grotesque avatar of the tree into the world.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: White Rotation.
Dozens of whirlpools formed behind her, and branches extended like hands from their centers.
The hands gripped the air, spinning like corkscrews, causing the faces of Siok to twist in agony.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Ugh!
Their hidden codes were completely shattered.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Reversing the laws.
Wizard's integrated sensory system included the reverse side of the world.
The hidden code may not have adhered to human logic, but neither did Wizard's.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Then, what is it?
To Siok's question, Havitz answered.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: No one knows.
It was a world only Wizard could perceive, and from that world sprang boundless creativity.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: …Impressive work.
Havitz didn't know who had raised Wizard as a weapon, but he felt a shiver at its sheer efficiency.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: She's still young. She lacks experience. Physically, her strikes aren't even that strong. But…

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: The potential.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: That limitless possibility.
It was as if something destined to bloom in the distant future had been drawn forth into this very moment.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Do you really want to kill me that badly?
The fact that she was an unstoppable existence only made Havitz feel a sense of kinship with her.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Heh heh heh.
There couldn't be a more fitting finale to his life.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Let's play!
The muscles of Havitz's face were pushed back, revealing the true face of Satan.
Faster than the Siok, sharper than a blade, his claws grazed Wizard's cheek.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: First Void Rotation.
Wizard clasped her hands together at her core, and a massive ball of compressed air plummeted downward.
Boom!
The force was enough to shake the ground, but Satan only bent his back slightly in response.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Heh. Your creativity is certainly something. It's impossible to dodge and impossible to block. But…
As Havitz's dull, amber eyes fixed forward, an overwhelming wave of malice surged outward.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: This weak? That's disappointing.
Wizard's facial muscles twitched for the first time as the Wandering of 6 o'clock slammed into the ground.

blocks.speaker: Wandering of 6 o'clock
blocks.content: Now's the time!
Assuming the effort of 200 years, the martial artist's kick drove toward Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Air Shield.
The air barrier burst with a pop as the man's shin slammed into the child's side.
Her ribs all shattered.
Without even groaning, Wizard flew sideways, using one foot to brake herself.
The pain of her spilling organs caused her legs to give out, and one knee touched the ground.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Should I heal myself?
Normally, unless death was imminent, attacking would take priority.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: But I feel like I'm dying.
Her body's reaction suggested her heart would stop in about three seconds from the trauma.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I don't know. Should I gamble?
Despite countless trial-and-error scenarios with Shirone, there were still limits to her experience.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: There's no other way.
As the Void God was reabsorbed into the world, her injuries began to heal rapidly.
Havitz wasn't one to miss the only opportunity when Wizard's chance to counterattack disappeared.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Still young.
Though death lingered in Satan's mind, assumptions held no meaning in the face of chaos.
Havitz's body, now crimson as though stripped of skin, swung both arms at once.
Just before Wizard's face could be smashed—

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: What?
Havitz—or rather, Satan—felt something he had never experienced before.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: What is this?
Wizard was suddenly far away, and two distinct lines were etched into the ground.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: I was pushed back.
What had hit him?
Lowering his gaze, Havitz realized only his solar plexus had returned to the color of human flesh.
In the sky, Wizard rose, bending one leg and spreading her arms wide.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Void Creation.
From the centerline of her body—her brow, throat, chest, navel, genitals, and perineum—her chakras began to glow.
Then, tens of thousands of chakras opened, and light radiated from Wizard's body.
Havitz did not define it.
Therefore, he could not be caught off guard, nor fall into traps of thought. But the one thing he had overlooked…

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Genius.
It was the transcendent creativity of a human capable of imagining even things that did not exist in the universe.
As sprouts of light grew along Wizard's arms, all 11 Siok charged.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Kill her!
The sprouts rapidly transformed into branches, fractally expanding and filling the sky.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: She's still imagining.
Destruction. Change. Creation.
The mechanism of this destructive-creative cycle spiraled infinitely, and the avatar of the Void God blossomed once more.
Its form was…

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: …What?
It didn't exist in the universe—a form so shocking that just looking at it felt like it could stop your heart.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Hyperform.
Havitz thought to himself:

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Form is everything. Every phenomenon in the universe begins from forms—waves smaller than subatomic particles.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Then what phenomenon does that form create?
As if answering Havitz's question, Wizard slowly clasped her hands together and made a hand sign.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Boundaryless Void.
The eleven Siok members saw crimson petals bloom swiftly before their eyes, like bursts of light.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Hyperwave.
The fruit of imagination was sublimated into flowers, which began to bloom from the heads of all the Siok members.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: It's unstoppable.
It was something Wizard alone had imagined—something that did not adhere to the physical laws of the universe.
The Nihility of 12 o'clock gritted his teeth.

blocks.speaker: Nihility of 12 o'clock
blocks.content: Damn it…
Everything reset.
Bang!
As his head exploded, the remaining Siok opened their eyes wide in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: She targeted 12 o'clock from the beginning.
Even in the surreal reality of ten Siok members surviving, Wizard remained calm.
To her, it wasn't surreal at all.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Can we end this now?
As Wizard landed on the ground and made a hand sign, the Void God quivered and then vanished.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Cherry Blossom.
Death.

blocks.speaker: Satan
blocks.content: Graaaaah!
Sensing his annihilation, Satan's body swelled to an enormous size, and crimson flowers blossomed across his form.
The frenzied Siok charged forward.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Satan, watch over us!
Wizard traced a circle with her finger, aimed at Satan, and pierced her thumb through the circle.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Void Burst.
Thousands of flowers bloomed simultaneously, and an indescribable shock reduced Satan's body to a smaller, diminished form.

blocks.speaker: Satan
blocks.content: Graaaah! GRAAAAH!
Shock, restraint, suppression, transformation—all forms of physical forces worked to reduce Satan back into Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: No!
As the Siok panicked, Wizard rushed toward the half-naked and staggering Havitz.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'll kill him here.
The time until Satan's annihilation? One second? Maybe 1.5 seconds?

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I've done it.
It was an incredibly short amount of time.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'm going to kill Satan.
But it was enough time for a human to dream, even when success was within their grasp.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: If I kill Satan…
There was no greed.
Even if the entire world were offered to her, it would mean nothing.
The only sweet thought that crossed her mind was:

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Shirone… brother.
Even as her mind was forcibly elevated into the realm of adulthood, her desires remained small.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Would he praise me? Would he say I did well?
But then, a faint voice of desire.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: What?
At the moment Havitz vanished from her perception, her pupils trembled in shock.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: What am I doing here?
Havitz, realizing Vanishing had activated through her momentary doubt, exuded killing intent.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Grrrhhh!
As he pushed off the ground and charged, Wizard couldn't pull herself out of her confusion.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Why? Why now?
She didn't know.
But her extraordinary resilience snapped her out of her ignorance and restored her to her original state.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Havitz!
As soon as Wizard recognized him, Havitz's fist struck her abdomen.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Ugh…!
An adult's fist embedded into the body of a child left her breathless.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Guh! Guh!
The Siok swarmed like demons.
But Havitz was faster, and his longsword rested against Wizard's neck.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Satan! You must kill her!
Wizard still couldn't move, and the fact that Havitz hadn't already struck her down filled the Siok with dread.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Please kill her now! There won't be another chance.
But Havitz thought differently.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: …Hah.
The man who had narrowly escaped death exhaled deeply toward the sky, then spoke to Wizard.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Are you okay? That was a bit rough.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: …Kill me.
She had lost her sense of self.
She didn't think of fighting again or catching him off guard for a surprise strike.
The moment she entertained such thoughts, Havitz would disappear from her grasp.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: The fight's over. So kill me.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Hmmm.
Havitz removed the blade from Wizard's neck and collapsed onto the ground as if his legs had given out.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: That was close. You're remarkable. To extract one result from pure chaos—that's something even I can't do.
Because it was a contradiction.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Kill me! Just kill me!

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: But contradictions aren't a natural state. Failing to maintain it for longer was your undoing. In five years—no, maybe even one year—you could kill me perfectly.
Wizard slowly straightened her back.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: …One day is enough.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Maybe. Honestly, I don't even know what you're thinking. Did Shirone send you?
Wizard gritted her teeth.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: I'd rather die.
Facing her master's disappointment, dying here seemed the better option.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: What do you want? If you plan to let me live, then leave.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Planning to kill yourself?
Havitz stared silently at the child biting her lip, then asked himself:

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: What is this girl to me?
He didn't care to find the answer, but one thing was certain—he didn't want Wizard to die.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: It's strange.
Even meeting the 4 Gustavs wasn't this thrilling.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Ah, I see.
He just wanted to play more.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: This girl sees me.
Perhaps even offers him some form of comfort.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Should I suggest tormenting people together? No… I'll ask her to fight alongside Shirone instead. That would be fun too…
Wizard's inner voice reached him.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: She hates me.
Sadness briefly flickered in Havitz's eyes before he stood up slowly and said,

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: If you ever want to kill me, come find me.

blocks.speaker: Siok
blocks.content: Satan! No!
As the Siok sank into the ground, Wizard tilted her head in confusion.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Find you?

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Yeah. Of course, just wanting to kill me won't be enough, but… you'll try anyway, won't you? Better that than dying.
Wizard fell silent, lost in thought, but Havitz seemed to already understand.

blocks.speaker: Havitz
blocks.content: Today was fun.
Even as Havitz disappeared into the forest, Wizard stayed rooted in place for a long time.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Sniff…
The hypnosis broke.

blocks.speaker: Wizard
blocks.content: Waaaaah! Waaaaah!
Having returned to her seven-year-old self, Wizard burst into tears, sobbing uncontrollably.
